title: malus communis subsp. sylvestris
intro: Malus communis subsp. sylvestris, also known as the wild apple or crab apple, is a subspecies of the apple tree that grows in the wild and is native to Europe. The leaves, bark, and fruit of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Malus communis subsp. sylvestris include its ability to aid in digestion, lower blood pressure, and boost the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, or used as a topical ointment or salve to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
